dependencies: Reliance on independent brokers for distribution
Dependence on managing general agents and program administrators
Reliance on third-party reinsurers and retrocessionaires
Dependence on third-party technology service providers including cloud providers and SaaS solutions
Reliance on Lloyd's distribution network and licenses
risks: Broker concentration risk - inability of brokers to fulfill contractual obligations
Technology service provider failures or cybersecurity incidents
Reinsurer credit risk - failure of reinsurers to meet obligations
Third-party service provider failures could disrupt operations
Vendor management risks related to information security
Counterparty credit risk with reinsurers and retrocessionaires
Reinsurance arrangements do not relieve primary obligations to insureds
Third parties may not adequately manage their risks or commit fraud
Managing general agents may exceed underwriting authorities
Technology failures and cyber attacks on business partners and service providers

Anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism legislation
APRA prudential supervision in Australia
Climate-related financial disclosure requirements (TCFD, CSRD)

GSE eligibility requirements (PMIERs) for mortgage insurers

Changes to FHA mortgage insurance programs
Basel III Capital Accord implementation

Data protection and GDPR compliance requirements

Sanctions targeting Russian military and defense systems
Risk of writing business in sanctioned jurisdictions
Impact on marine and energy lines of business
Economic trade sanctions administered by U.S. Treasury OFAC

Regulatory risks from sanctions on Russia and Iran
